sections.title: Test coverage analysis
sections.content: **Zero test infrastructure exists.** No test runner is installed (`package.json` has no `vitest`/`jest`/`@testing-library/*`), and there isn't a single `*.test.*`/`*.spec.*` file in the repo. Every gap below is therefore "untested," but I've flagged the highest-risk ones — `self-prompt.ts` in particular, since it lets the model rewrite its own system prompt with no human approval gate.

sections.title: 0. Setup gap (blocking everything else)
1. `src/lib/self-prompt.ts` — highest risk, zero coverage
2. `src/lib/note-extraction.ts` — no coverage
3. `src/lib/rag.ts` — formatting helpers untested
4. `src/lib/vault.ts` — no coverage
5. `src/lib/embeddings.ts` — no coverage
6. `src/lib/anthropic.ts` — no coverage
7. `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` — the biggest integration gap
8. `src/app/api/onboarding/route.ts` — no coverage
9. `src/app/api/auth/callback/route.ts` — no coverage
10. Lower priority (small, but currently 0%)
sections.content:
- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `cn()`: dedupe conflicting Tailwind classes (e.g. `cn("p-2", "p-4")` → `"p-4"`). One-liner test, cheap win.
- `src/lib/prompts.ts` — `buildChatSystemPrompt`/`buildOnboardingSystemPrompt`/`buildNoteExtractionPrompt`: pure string assembly, verify section ordering (`IMMUTABLE_CORE` always first) and the `"[no specific notes retrieved for this turn]"` fallback text appears when arrays are empty.
- Components (`ChatInterface.tsx`, `ChatInput.tsx`, `MessageList.tsx`, `MessageBubble.tsx`, `AuroraBrain.tsx`) have no rendering tests at all — out of scope for this pass since no `@testing-library/react`/jsdom is installed yet, but flagging since they own all the SSE-consumption logic on the client side, which is a mirror-image gap to the server route above.

---

sections.title: Priority order if picking a starting point
sections.content:
1. Install Vitest (blocking).
2. `self-prompt.ts` — safety-critical, self-modifying behavior, currently the least guarded code path in the app.
3. `api/chat/route.ts` — the history-filtering and tool-loop logic is subtle enough that a refactor could silently break it today with no signal.
4. `note-extraction.ts` / `vault.ts` — governs what gets permanently written to Hudson's vault.
5. Everything else.

rawOutputPreview:
## Test coverage analysis

**Zero test infrastructure exists.** No test runner is installed (`package.json` has no `vitest`/`jest`/`@testing-library/*`), and there isn't a single `*.test.*`/`*.spec.*` file in the repo. Every gap below is therefore "untested," but I've flagged the highest-risk ones — `self-prompt.ts` in particular, since it lets the model rewrite its own system prompt with no human approval gate.

### 0. Setup gap (blocking everything else)

This is an ESM Next.js 15 project, so **Vitest** is the natural fit (fast, native ESM, easy Next path aliases).

```bash
pnpm add -D vitest @vitejs/plugin-react vite-tsconfig-paths @vitest/coverage-v8
```

```ts
// vitest.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "vitest/config";
import tsconfigPaths from "vite-tsconfig-paths";

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [tsconfigPaths()],
  test: { environment: "node", globals: true },
});
```

Add `"test": "vitest run"` to `package.json` scripts. All skeletons below assume this.

---

## 1. `src/lib/self-prompt.ts` — highest risk, zero coverage

This is the autonomous self-edit path (no approval gate, per `CLAUDE.md`'s own description of the feature). Untested branches:

- `getActivePrompt`: no state row → fallback; state row with null `active_version` → fallback; version row missing/empty `body` → fallback; DB throws → fallback (catch swallows and logs).
- `isSelfEditEnabled`: no row → defaults `true`; explicit `false`; DB throws → **fails closed to `false`** (comment says so, but nothing verifies it).
- `applyEdit`: kill-switch off → rejected without writing; empty/whitespace body → rejected; body over `MAX_BODY_CHARS` → rejected; `maxRow` null → `nextVersion` becomes `1`; insert error → `ok:false`, state not flipped; upsert error → `ok:false` after insert already succeeded (inconsistent state — worth a comment/test even if not fixed).
- `revertTo`: non-integer/`NaN`/`<1` version → rejected before any DB call; target version doesn't exist → rejected; DB throws → c
